DEMOTION Value: This is not actually trait, but it i5 how decrease in the Rank Trait is listed in the Life Path Each Demotion lowers tne level of the character' Rank Trait by one minimum of number in parentheses subtracts the number from the level of the character' Rank Trait, minimum of 1.
acter only in certain limited circumstances_ only restricts small part of the body; such as single hand or foot: 2-point disability restricts the character fairly often or affects large part of the body; such as a entire arm or leg; or an entire system; such as the digestive tract or circulatory system. 3 point dis ability aftects the character constantly or affects the entire body:
ELEMENTAL PHENOTYPE Cost: character with this trait is trueborn elemental, bred specifically by Clan scientists for the rigors of battle-armor com- bat. Elementals are huge compared with other warriors, standing from 2 to 2.5 meters tall and disproportionately muscular, mak- ing them massively heavy as well_ Elementals primarily act as elite intantry shock troops; but they also serve in variety of other roles, such as security and marines. Elementals who wash out of training most often end up in the laborer caste where their strength and endurance make them excellent workers_ The   primary advantages Elementals receive are modified Attribute thresholds in the Life Path (STR +2 BOD +1 DEX -1) and modified Attribute maximums as noted the Attribute Maximums Table 56). Because they have been carefully designed and bred over dozens of generations t0 operate battle armor; Elementals are considered to have the Natural Aptitude Trait (p. 84) for all the skills in the Elemental Field_ In addition_ all Elementals have the Toughness Trait (p. 87) at no Character Point cost.
DEPENDENTS Value: 1-2 character with this trait has one or more vulnerable rela tives or other dependents who make perfect target for kid nappers ad other villains The value of the trait is based on the wulnerability of the dependents: If the dependent is reason ably competent adult who lives in fairly secure Iocation the trait IS worth point. If the dependents are very young or infirm and/or tney are particularly vulnerable to predation (for exam: ple, they live alone on secluded farm, Or travel frequently with no escort), the trait is worth 2 points_
DIBABLED (multiple trait) Value: 1-3 character who is disabled suffers some form of affliction that limits him some way: The exact definition and effects of the disability are up to the player and the gamemaster: The point value of the disability is based on the amount of disability inflicted by this trait: 1-point disability limits the char-
